name: Fuentegelmes - Torete River Loop
name_original: Fuentegelmes — circular por el valle del río Torete
description: Hike the Fuentegelmes circular route through the Torete river valley, a moderate 6.7-mile trail with historical sites and diverse scenery.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Arcos De Jalon, a municipality in Soria, Spain, traverse a diverse landscape characterized by the Jalón River valley, high plains, and riverine vegas. The region features natural elements such as the Gorges of the Jalón River and the unique karst wetland of Laguna de Judes. Hikers can also explore El Sabinar de Solorio, one of Europe's most extensive juniper forests. This area offers a variety of terrain suitable for easy walks, often incorporating historical attractions. Best easy hiking trails around Arcos De Jalon The most popular easy hiking route is Medinaceli - Camino del Cid Trail, a 1.6 miles (2.5 km) trail that takes 41 minutes to complete. This route offers a glimpse into the historic Camino del Cid, passing through the ancient town of Medinaceli. Another top favourite among local hikers is Santa María de Huerta — circular, an easy 2.9 miles (4.6 km) path. This circular route provides views of the surrounding countryside and takes approximately 1 hour 14 minutes. Local hikers also love the Medinaceli – Medinaceli loop from Medinaceli, a 3.8 miles (6.2 km) trail leading through the historic town of Medinaceli, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Hiking around Arcos De Jalon is defined by the Jalón River valley, its gorges, and extensive juniper forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on easy hiking trails. The routes in Arcos De Jalon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 6 reviews. More than 60 hikers have used komoot to explore Arcos De Jalon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Arcos de Jalón?
A: There are over 15 easy hiking trails around Arcos de Jalón, offering a variety of landscapes suitable for relaxed walks. In total, the region boasts more than 40 hiking routes across all difficulty levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes around Arcos de Jalón?
A: Easy hikes in Arcos de Jalón traverse a diverse landscape. You'll find paths winding through the lush &lt;em&gt;Jalón River valley&lt;/em&gt;, across high plains, and along picturesque riverine vegas. The region also features unique natural elements like the &lt;em&gt;Gorges of the Jalón River&lt;/em&gt; and extensive juniper forests such as &lt;em&gt;El Sabinar de Solorio&lt;/em&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy circular routes in the area?
A: Yes, Arcos de Jalón offers several easy circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1265282684/santa-maria-de-huerta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Huerta — circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.6 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcos de Jalón loop from Arcos de Jalón&lt;/a&gt; provides a pleasant 3.9 km walk. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24540973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli – Medinaceli loop from Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.2 km and takes you through the historic town.

Q: What natural features can I see on easy walks around Arcos de Jalón?
A: Easy walks in the area often lead you to impressive natural features. You can explore sections of the &lt;em&gt;Gorges of the Jalón River&lt;/em&gt;, known for their rocky walls and birdlife. The unique karst wetland of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5999892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Judes&lt;/a&gt; is another notable sight, though it's a temporary lagoon. Additionally, you might encounter the ancient trees of &lt;em&gt;El Sabinar de Solorio&lt;/em&gt;, one of Europe's largest juniper forests.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible on easy trails?
A: While some waterfalls require more challenging routes, you can find beautiful water features in the region. The &lt;em&gt;Gorges of the Jalón River&lt;/em&gt; area is known for its 'Ruta de las Cascadas'. Highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6020877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Cuerda&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5999948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Chorroneras of Chaorna&lt;/a&gt; are part of the region's natural beauty, offering scenic spots to enjoy.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore on easy hikes near Arcos de Jalón?
A: Many easy hikes incorporate historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1263799305/medinaceli-camino-del-cid-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli - Camino del Cid Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers a glimpse into the historic Camino del Cid and passes through the ancient town of Medinaceli, known for its Roman triumphal arch. You can also find views of the ruins of &lt;em&gt;Montuenga Castle&lt;/em&gt;, an ancient medieval fortress, on some routes.

Q: Is Arcos de Jalón a good destination for family-friendly easy hikes?
A: Yes, Arcos de Jalón is well-suited for family-friendly easy hikes. The region's gentle terrain along the Jalón River valley and its numerous short, well-defined paths make it ideal for families with children. Many routes are relatively flat and offer interesting natural and historical sights to keep everyone engaged.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Arcos de Jalón?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Arcos de Jalón, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscape, the blend of natural beauty with historical sites, and the accessibility of easy trails that allow for a relaxed exploration of the region.

Q: Are there options for parking near the trailheads for easy hikes?
A: Yes, many trailheads for easy hikes in Arcos de Jalón and surrounding villages offer convenient parking options. It's generally easy to find places to park your car, especially in the main towns and near popular starting points, allowing for easy access to the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hiking in Arcos de Jalón?
A: Arcos de Jalón experiences a continental Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for easy hiking, with mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for crisp, clear walks.

Q: Can I find cafes or restaurants near the starting points of easy trails?
A: Yes, in Arcos de Jalón and nearby towns like Medinaceli and Somaén, you'll find cafes, bars, and restaurants where you can grab a refreshment or a meal before or after your hike. These establishments are often conveniently located within the villages, close to common trail starting points.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the easy hiking trails in Arcos de Jalón?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Arcos de Jalón region. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb other hikers or wildlife.
tips: The trail reaches the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción in the small town of Fuentegelmes in the fertile landscape of the Bordecorex river valley.
Path through the fertile landscape of the Torete river valley between the towns of Bordecorex and Fuentegelmes.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Fuentegelmes — circular through the Torete river valley trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as a moderate hiking route. It offers a balanced challenge suitable for hikers with some experience.

faqs.question: Is the Fuentegelmes — circular through the Torete river valley a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as its name suggests, this is a circular route, meaning you will end your hike at the same point where you started.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Fuentegelmes — circular through the Torete river valley trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, it is generally advisable to look for parking within the village of Fuentegelmes, near the trailhead.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail follows the picturesque Torete River valley, featuring diverse landscapes. You'll see areas with typical cereal crops like wheat and barley, stony fields, and hillsides where sheep graze. The path also passes by the "puente del Hocino" (Hocino bridge) near Fuentegelmes.

faqs.question: What historical sites and landmarks can I see along the Fuentegelmes trail?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in history. You can see remnants of High Middle Ages watchtowers along the valley. In Fuentegelmes itself, look for a large stone cross, the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, an impressive lintel, and a distinctive concave sundial on a hermitage roof.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Torete river valley trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, avoiding the heat of summer or potential winter conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Fuentegelmes — circular through the Torete river valley trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, many trails in rural Spain are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock like the sheep mentioned in the research.

faqs.question: Are permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Fuentegelmes — circular through the Torete river valley trail.

faqs.question: Is there water available along the trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of specific water sources along the trail. It is highly recommended to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of your hike, especially on warmer days.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, bring comfortable hiking shoes, adequate water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a camera to capture the scenic Torete River Valley and historical sights. A map or GPS device is also advisable.

faqs.question: How long is the Fuentegelmes — circular through the Torete river valley trail and how much elevation gain does it have?
faqs.answer: The trail is approximately 10.8 kilometers (6.7 miles) long, with an elevation gain of about 165 meters (541 feet).

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, Fuentegelmes
